pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states save location; verified in setup_task.sh (line 13), export_result.sh (line 9), and verifier.py
Task description explicitly specifies all 5 layers with names and purposes; verified in verifier.py line 64
Task description states: 'Draw the room perimeter rectangle from (0, 0) to (3000, 2400)'; calculated width=3000mm, height=2400mm
Task description: 'inset 150mm from walls: rectangle from (150, 150) to (2850, 2250)'; calculated width=2850-150=2700mm, height=2250-150=2100mm; verified: 3000-2*150=2700, 2400-2*150=2100
Task specifies: vertical lines at x=[450,750,1050,1350,1650,1950,2250,2550] (8 lines) + horizontal lines at y=[450,750,1050,1350,1650,1950] (6 lines) = 14 total grid lines

privileged_info_summary: The tile_floor_layout@1 task is a synthetic, procedurally-specified CAD drawing task with no external data dependencies. The task requires an AI agent to create a technical drawing in LibreCAD with specific geometric constraints: a 3000x2400mm room with a 2700x2100mm tile border (150mm inset), and a 300x300mm grid containing 14 lines (8 vertical, 6 horizontal). Output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/restroom_tile_layout.dxf with 5 mandatory layers: WALLS, BORDER, TILES, DIMENSIONS, and TEXT. All metadata claims about dimensions, layers, and grid density are accurate and consistent with the task specification.
